title: selaginella praestans
intro: Selaginella praestans, also known as the "golden club moss," is a type of spore-producing plant that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, Selaginella praestans is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
